intent: Remove proven-dead frontend visual code while preserving behavior, layout, and responsive sizing.
assumptions: Backend and unrelated dirty worktree changes are out of scope.
Files are removable only when production references and meaningful test references are absent.
Active semantic tokens used by Share, Upload, and other pages remain in place.
risks: Static reachability can miss dynamic imports.
Token cleanup can affect active pages when names are shared.
Decorative but active components are not dead code.
steps: Scan imports and active source references.
Delete confirmed orphan components/assets and orphan tests.
Remove CSS glyphs and tokens tied exclusively to deleted components.
Run TypeScript, focused tests, neuromorphic scan, fluid sizing, lint, and build.
Record the cleanup in constraints and quality score.
